title: Add semantic memory dependencies and DB bootstrap primitives
description: Add required runtime dependencies and create the initial memory backend primitives needed to open a shared database, enable WAL mode, load sqlite-vec, resolve/cache the embedding model, and report basic stats.
acceptance_criteria: `pyproject.toml` includes required memory dependencies in main project dependencies.
`src/agent_takt/memory.py` defines idempotent DB/bootstrap helpers needed by later memory operations.
`init_db()` creates the DB path, enables WAL mode, and prepares schema/extension loading without requiring later CLI work to duplicate setup.
A basic `stats()` implementation can inspect the initialized DB state.

handoff_summary.completed: Added onnxruntime>=1.17, sqlite-vec>=0.1, tokenizers>=0.19, numpy>=1.26 to pyproject.toml [project.dependencies]. Created src/agent_takt/memory.py with: init_db() (creates DB path, enables WAL mode, loads sqlite-vec extension, creates entries + vectors virtual tables — idempotent), stats() (total entries, per-namespace counts, db_path), the full public API (add_entry, search, ingest_file, delete_entry), and all private helpers: _download_model() (idempotent download of BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 ONNX files from HuggingFace to ~/.cache/agent-takt/models/), _embed() (tokenizers + onnxruntime inference with mean-pooling + L2 normalisation, returns sqlite_vec.serialize_float32 blob), _open_conn() / _load_sqlite_vec(), and text chunking helpers (_chunk_markdown, _chunk_text, _split_if_large).
handoff_summary.remaining: Other feature-tree beads handle: takt memory CLI subcommand, runner env-var injection (TAKT_CMD/AGENT_MEMORY_DB/AGENT_TAKT_FEATURE_ROOT_ID), guardrail template updates, onboarding.py DB bootstrap call, planner.py spec ingestion, and SKILL.md replacements.
handoff_summary.risks: 1. HuggingFace download: model files at BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5/onnx/model.onnx must be reachable at first init — air-gapped environments will fail. 2. sqlite-vec enable_load_extension requires a SQLite build with extension support; some restricted Linux builds may not have it. 3. The KNN search does not push namespace filtering into the vec virtual table — over-fetching (limit*20) could miss entries if namespace is very sparse relative to the whole table; tester should verify.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify: init_db idempotency (call twice, expect no error), add_entry + search round-trip in global/feature namespaces, ingest_file deduplication (same file ingested twice produces same count as once), stats() returns correct counts, that search without --namespace returns results from multiple namespaces with namespace field present, and that delete_entry raises ValueError for missing ids.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: src/agent_takt/memory.py is fully implemented here. Sibling beads should import from it but not modify it. If any sibling bead lists memory.py in expected_files they should be scoped to additive imports only.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. Full module implemented in this bead (not just stubs): since all six public functions depend on the same private infrastructure (_embed, _open_conn, chunking helpers), splitting across beads would force multiple bead passes on the same file. Implementing the complete module here removes that churn. 2. Module-level ORT session + tokenizer cache with threading.Lock: each worker agent is a separate subprocess so cross-process cache sharing is not needed; the lock protects against intra-process concurrent threads (e.g. if the CLI ever adds async paths). 3. KNN over-fetch + Python-side namespace filter: sqlite-vec vec0 does not natively support predicate pushdown on joined columns; over-fetching (limit * 20) is simple and correct at expected DB sizes (thousands of entries). A vec0 with PARTITION KEY could improve this but is not needed now. 4. Separate entries (metadata) and vectors (vec0 virtual) tables linked by rowid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT: this is the idiomatic sqlite-vec pattern and allows clean deletion of both rows atomically. 5. Binary embedding format via sqlite_vec.serialize_float32: preferred over JSON-array strings for compatibility across sqlite-vec versions. 6. Mean-pool + L2-normalise for embeddings: handles both (batch, seq, dim) and (batch, dim) outputs so the same code works whether the ONNX export includes pre-pooled outputs or not.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Test init_db idempotency: call on fresh path, verify WAL mode is set (PRAGMA journal_mode returns 'wal'), call again and expect no error or schema duplication. Test add_entry + search round-trip: add a known string to 'global', search for it, expect top result distance < 0.1 and correct text/namespace/source fields. Test namespace isolation: add to 'global' and 'feature:X', search with namespace='global' should not return 'feature:X' results. Test search without namespace: should return results from all namespaces, sorted by distance, each with a namespace field. Test ingest_file: ingest a small .md file, count returned equals number of non-empty chunks; ingest again, count should be 0 (dedup). Test delete_entry: add then delete by UUID, verify subsequent search does not return it; delete non-existent UUID should raise ValueError. Test stats: after adds, verify total_entries and by_namespace match expectations. Regression: existing tests in test_orchestrator.py and test_cli_*.py must still pass — memory.py is a new module with no cross-wiring yet.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: 1. No connection pooling — each public function opens and closes its own connection. Acceptable for CLI use; not ideal for high-frequency programmatic use. 2. Deduplication in ingest_file requires a search() call per chunk — O(n*m) for n chunks against m existing entries; fine for doc-sized files. 3. HuggingFace download uses urllib with no retry or progress reporting — a poor network will silently stall. 4. Model loading is eager on first embed call and blocks the lock during load (~1–3 s for ONNX session creation). 5. The ONNX model path is hardcoded as onnx/model.onnx relative to the repo root — if HuggingFace restructures the BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 repo this breaks.

conflict_risks: src/agent_takt/memory.py is fully implemented here. Sibling beads should import from it but not modify it. If any sibling bead lists memory.py in expected_files they should be scoped to additive imports only.

execution_history.summary: Added four memory runtime dependencies to pyproject.toml and created src/agent_takt/memory.py implementing the full semantic memory backend with DB bootstrap primitives.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
